purpose.question: What is the purpose of this solution?
purpose.answer: The embedded turso database for the CoperativeAI workspace app. It stores the app's own working data — products and their strategies and deliverables, solutions (including their linked GitHub repository), repositories, work items with their commercial fields, sprints, team members and roles, test cases, feature designs, system settings, AI provider settings (key aliases only, never key values), and the AI policies that gate every AI call (per work item and per Product). It stores no user accounts and no secret values: API keys and the GitHub token live in the OS credential store, and the app has no logins.

hosting.question: Where will it be hosted, deployed, or distributed?
hosting.answer: Embedded inside the CoperativeAI application — opened in-process as a local file, not a separate hosted server.

frameworks.question: What frameworks, libraries, or UI toolkit should it use?

fileLayout.question: What is the folder and file layout of this solution's repo?

testing.question: What test framework should be used, and what kinds of tests?

commands.question: The commands the AI should use to work with this repo.

scaffold.note: Everything comes from the turso-embedded boilerplate in _forms/boilerplates.json — no overrides. It has no 'deploy' command since the database ships embedded inside the CoperativeAI application build, per notes.mustNotDo below.

baseline.question: Start from a named security baseline?

rules.question: Any extra security rules for this solution?
rules.note: Trimmed to what the embedded-database-baseline doesn't already cover (it already requires: no hard-coded credentials, parameterised queries, encryption at rest, least privilege). Multi-factor auth and connection strings don't apply to an in-process embedded file, so they were dropped.
entries: Regularly update the turso crate to pick up security fixes
Enable auditing/logging of database activity, without logging user data
Regularly back up the database file and test restore procedures

resources.question: What infrastructure resources does this solution need?

provisioning.question: How is infrastructure created, and by whom?
provisioning.answer: Part of the CoperativeAI application build process — no separate provisioning.

environments.question: What environments exist, and which may the AI deploy to?
environments.answer: dev (AI may deploy), production (people deploy after review).

settings.question: What named settings and secrets does this solution need at build or run time?

pipeline.question: What CI/CD pipeline should this solution have?
pipeline.answer: No separate pipeline — the database is built and tested as part of the CoperativeAI application's own pipeline (see ../CoperativeAI/application-spec.json).

mustNotDo.question: Anything this solution must NOT do / cannot deploy?
mustNotDo.answer: Only deploy with the main application — it has no independent deployment.

limits.question: Any limits (rate limits, payload size, memory, startup time, supported platforms)?
